```{r}
library(ecodist)  # for MRM (multiple regression on distance matrices)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(dplyr)
library(marmap)

# Create output subdirectory using full path
output_dir <- "./geoVenv_eDNA"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  cat("✓ Created output directory:", output_dir, "\n")
} else {
  cat("✓ Output directory already exists:", output_dir, "\n")
}
```

# Data Prep
```{r}
# Verify required objects exist
if(!exists("community_4th_root_edna")) {
  stop("community_4th_root_edna not found. Please run dbRDA script first.")
}
if(!exists("metadata_edna")) {
  stop("metadata_edna not found. Please ensure metadata is loaded.")
}

cat("Using existing community_4th_root_edna object\n")
cat("Community data:", nrow(community_4th_root_edna), "samples x", ncol(community_4th_root_edna), "ASVs\n")
cat("Sample alignment check:", identical(rownames(community_4th_root_edna), rownames(metadata_edna)), "\n")

#================= ENVIRONMENTAL VARIABLES SETUP ===============
# Select available environmental variables
env_vars_available_edna <- intersect(c("Depth", "Visibility_std_rank", "pp_mean"), 
                                     colnames(metadata_edna))

env_vars_mantel_edna <- metadata_edna %>%
  dplyr::select(all_of(env_vars_available_edna)) %>%
  na.omit()

cat("Environmental variables included:", ncol(env_vars_mantel_edna), "\n")
cat("Variables:", paste(colnames(env_vars_mantel_edna), collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Samples with complete environmental data:", nrow(env_vars_mantel_edna), "\n")

# Match community and metadata to complete environmental data
env_samples_edna <- rownames(env_vars_mantel_edna)
community_complete_edna <- community_4th_root_edna[env_samples_edna, ]
metadata_complete_edna <- metadata_edna[env_samples_edna, ]

cat("Final dataset dimensions:", nrow(community_complete_edna), "samples x", ncol(community_complete_edna), "ASVs\n")

# Check correlations between environmental variables
env_cor_edna <- cor(env_vars_mantel_edna)
cat("\n=== ENVIRONMENTAL VARIABLE CORRELATIONS ===\n")
print(round(env_cor_edna, 3))

# Flag high correlations
high_cor_pairs_edna <- which(abs(env_cor_edna) > 0.7 & env_cor_edna != 1, arr.ind = TRUE)
if (nrow(high_cor_pairs_edna) > 0) {
  cat("⚠️ High correlations detected between some variables\n")
  for (i in 1:nrow(high_cor_pairs_edna)) {
    var1 <- rownames(env_cor_edna)[high_cor_pairs_edna[i,1]]
    var2 <- colnames(env_cor_edna)[high_cor_pairs_edna[i,2]]
    cat(sprintf("  %s vs %s: r = %.3f\n", var1, var2, env_cor_edna[high_cor_pairs_edna[i,1], high_cor_pairs_edna[i,2]]))
  }
} else {
  cat("✓ All correlations below 0.7\n")
}

# Standardize environmental variables for distance calculations
env_scaled_edna <- scale(env_vars_mantel_edna)
cat("✓ Environmental variables standardized\n")

#===========  DISTANCE MATRIX CALCULATIONS ============================

cat("\n=== DISTANCE MATRIX CALCULATIONS ===\n")

# 1. Community dissimilarity matrix (Bray-Curtis from 4th root transformed data)
community_dist_edna <- vegdist(community_complete_edna, method = "bray")
cat("✓ Community distance matrix calculated (Bray-Curtis from 4th root data)\n")

# 2. Geographic distance matrix using marmap (following metabarcoding approach)
cat("Calculating geographic distances using marmap least-cost approach...\n")

# Check for missing geographic coordinates
if (any(is.na(metadata_complete_edna[, c("Latitude", "Longitude")]))) {
  cat("⚠️ Missing geographic coordinates detected\n")
  complete_geo_edna <- complete.cases(metadata_complete_edna[, c("Latitude", "Longitude")])
  metadata_complete_edna <- metadata_complete_edna[complete_geo_edna, ]
  env_scaled_edna <- env_scaled_edna[complete_geo_edna, ]
  community_complete_edna <- community_complete_edna[complete_geo_edna, ]
  community_dist_edna <- vegdist(community_complete_edna, method = "bray")
  cat("Updated sample size:", nrow(community_complete_edna), "\n")
}

# Create coordinate sites dataframe for eDNA data
coord_sites_edna <- metadata_complete_edna[, c("Longitude", "Latitude")]
coord_sites_edna$depth <- metadata_complete_edna$Depth

cat("Coordinate sites for eDNA 18S:\n")
cat("Samples:", nrow(coord_sites_edna), "\n")
cat("Longitude range:", round(range(coord_sites_edna$Longitude), 3), "\n")
cat("Latitude range:", round(range(coord_sites_edna$Latitude), 3), "\n")

# Load bathymetric data (same region as other analyses)
cat("Loading NOAA bathymetric data...\n")
bathydata_edna <- getNOAA.bathy(lon1 = -95, lon2 = -90, lat1 = 27, lat2 = 30, resolution = 0.2)

# Create transition matrix based on bathymetric data
cat("Creating transition matrix...\n")
t_edna <- trans.mat(bathydata_edna, min.depth = -0.1)

# Prepare site coordinates for least-cost distance calculation
xy_sites_edna <- as.data.frame(coord_sites_edna[, c("Longitude", "Latitude")])

# Calculate least-cost distance matrix in kilometers
cat("Calculating least-cost distances...\n")
leastDist.km_edna <- lc.dist(t_edna, xy_sites_edna, res = "dist")

# Convert to standard distance object for further analysis
geographic_dist_edna <- leastDist.km_edna

# Calculate minimum observed distance
min_dist_edna <- min(leastDist.km_edna)
cat("✓ Geographic distance matrix calculated using marmap least-cost approach\n")
cat("Minimum distance between sites:", round(min_dist_edna, 2), "km\n")

#======= SAVE GEOGRAPHIC DISTANCE DATA =======

save(leastDist.km_edna, file = file.path(output_dir, 'leastDist.km_edna.Rdata'))

leastDist.km_edna_matrix <- as.matrix(leastDist.km_edna)
leastDist.km_edna_df <- as.data.frame(leastDist.km_edna_matrix)
write.csv(leastDist.km_edna_df, file = file.path(output_dir, "leastDist_km_edna.csv"), row.names = TRUE)

cat("✓ Geographic distance data saved as leastDist.km_edna.Rdata and leastDist_km_edna.csv\n")

# 3. Environmental distance matrix (Euclidean distance from scaled variables)
environmental_dist_edna <- dist(env_scaled_edna, method = "euclidean")
cat("✓ Environmental distance matrix calculated\n")

# Export environmental distance matrix as CSV
environmental_dist_edna_matrix <- as.matrix(environmental_dist_edna)
environmental_dist_edna_df <- as.data.frame(environmental_dist_edna_matrix)
write.csv(environmental_dist_edna_df, 
          file = file.path(output_dir, "environmental_dist_edna.csv"), 
          row.names = TRUE)

# Save all distance matrices as RData object for easy reloading
save(
  community_dist_edna,
  geographic_dist_edna,
  environmental_dist_edna,
  log_geographic_dist_edna,
  log_environmental_dist_edna,
  leastDist.km_edna,
  file = file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_distance_matrices.RData")
)


#============ LOG TRANSFORMATION OF DISTANCE MATRICES =====================

cat("\n=== LOG TRANSFORMATION OF DISTANCE MATRICES ===\n")

# Check for zero distances
zero_geo_edna <- sum(geographic_dist_edna == 0)
zero_env_edna <- sum(environmental_dist_edna == 0)

cat("Zero distances detected:\n")
cat("  Geographic:", zero_geo_edna, "\n")
cat("  Environmental:", zero_env_edna, "\n")

# Use log1p transformation (log(1+x)) to handle zeros
log_geographic_dist_edna <- as.dist(log1p(as.matrix(geographic_dist_edna)))
log_environmental_dist_edna <- as.dist(log1p(as.matrix(environmental_dist_edna)))

cat("✓ Log-transformed distance matrices created using log1p\n")

# Summary of all distance matrices
cat("\nDistance matrix summary:\n")
cat("- Community dissimilarity (Bray-Curtis): range", round(range(community_dist_edna), 3), "\n")
cat("- Geographic distance: range", round(range(geographic_dist_edna), 3), "\n") 
cat("- Environmental distance: range", round(range(environmental_dist_edna), 3), "\n")
cat("- Log geographic distance: range", round(range(log_geographic_dist_edna), 3), "\n")
cat("- Log environmental distance: range", round(range(log_environmental_dist_edna), 3), "\n")

```


# Mantel Tests
```{r Mantel Tests}

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("MANTEL TESTS - eDNA 18S\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

# 1. Environmental Mantel Test
cat("\n1. ENVIRONMENTAL MANTEL TEST\n")
cat("Community dissimilarity ~ Log(Environmental distance)\n")

mantel_env_edna <- vegan::mantel(community_dist_edna, log_environmental_dist_edna, 
                                 method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

cat("Results:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Mantel statistic (r): %.3f\n", mantel_env_edna$statistic))
cat(sprintf("  p-value: %.3f\n", mantel_env_edna$signif))

if (mantel_env_edna$signif < 0.05) {
  cat("  → SIGNIFICANT environmental-community relationship\n")
} else {
  cat("  → NO significant environmental-community relationship\n")
}

# 2. Geographic Mantel Test (Distance-Decay)
cat("\n2. GEOGRAPHIC MANTEL TEST (Distance-Decay)\n")
cat("Community dissimilarity ~ Log(Geographic distance)\n")

mantel_geo_edna <- vegan::mantel(community_dist_edna, log_geographic_dist_edna, 
                                 method = "pearson", permutations = 9999)

cat("Results:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Mantel statistic (r): %.3f\n", mantel_geo_edna$statistic))
cat(sprintf("  p-value: %.3f\n", mantel_geo_edna$signif))

if (mantel_geo_edna$signif < 0.05) {
  if (mantel_geo_edna$statistic > 0) {
    cat("  → SIGNIFICANT POSITIVE distance-decay: Community similarity decreases with distance\n")
  } else {
    cat("  → SIGNIFICANT NEGATIVE relationship: Community similarity increases with distance\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("  → NO significant distance-decay relationship\n")
}

```


# Partial Mantel Tests
```{r Partial Mantel Tests}

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("PARTIAL MANTEL TESTS - eDNA 18S\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

# 1. Partial Mantel: Environment controlling for Geography
cat("\n1. PARTIAL MANTEL TEST: ENVIRONMENT | GEOGRAPHY\n")
cat("Community dissimilarity ~ Log(Environmental distance) | Log(Geographic distance)\n")

partial_mantel_env_geo_edna <- vegan::mantel.partial(community_dist_edna, 
                                                      log_environmental_dist_edna, 
                                                      log_geographic_dist_edna,
                                                      method = "pearson", 
                                                      permutations = 9999)

cat("Results:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Partial Mantel statistic (r): %.3f\n", partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$statistic))
cat(sprintf("  p-value: %.3f\n", partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$signif))

if (partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$signif < 0.05) {
  cat("  → SIGNIFICANT environmental effects beyond geography\n")
  cat("  → Environmental filtering operates independently of spatial processes\n")
} else {
  cat("  → NO environmental effects beyond geography\n")
  cat("  → Environmental patterns may be confounded with spatial structure\n")
}

# 2. Partial Mantel: Geography controlling for Environment
cat("\n2. PARTIAL MANTEL TEST: GEOGRAPHY | ENVIRONMENT\n")
cat("Community dissimilarity ~ Log(Geographic distance) | Log(Environmental distance)\n")

partial_mantel_geo_env_edna <- vegan::mantel.partial(community_dist_edna, 
                                                      log_geographic_dist_edna, 
                                                      log_environmental_dist_edna,
                                                      method = "pearson", 
                                                      permutations = 9999)

cat("Results:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Partial Mantel statistic (r): %.3f\n", partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$statistic))
cat(sprintf("  p-value: %.3f\n", partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$signif))

if (partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$signif < 0.05) {
  if (partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$statistic > 0) {
    cat("  → SIGNIFICANT residual distance-decay: Geography matters beyond environment\n")
    cat("  → Suggests dispersal limitation or unmeasured spatial processes\n")
  } else {
    cat("  → SIGNIFICANT negative residual relationship\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("  → NO residual distance-decay after controlling for environment\n")
  cat("  → Geographic patterns fully explained by environmental gradients\n")
  cat("  → Supports environmental filtering over dispersal limitation\n")
}

```


# MRM Analysis
```{r MRM}

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("MULTIPLE REGRESSION ON DISTANCE MATRICES (MRM) - eDNA 18S\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

# Prepare data for MRM
bc_dist_vec_edna <- as.vector(community_dist_edna)
env_dist_vec_edna <- as.vector(log_environmental_dist_edna)
geo_dist_vec_edna <- as.vector(log_geographic_dist_edna)

# Create matched dataframe
mrm_data_edna <- data.frame(
  bc_dist = bc_dist_vec_edna,
  log_env_dist = env_dist_vec_edna,
  log_geo_dist = geo_dist_vec_edna
)

cat("MRM data prepared: ", nrow(mrm_data_edna), " pairwise comparisons\n")

# Run MRM analysis
cat("Running MRM with environmental and geographic predictors...\n")
mrm_result_edna <- ecodist::MRM(mrm_data_edna$bc_dist ~ mrm_data_edna$log_env_dist + mrm_data_edna$log_geo_dist, 
                                nperm = 9999)

# Display MRM results
cat("\n=== MRM RESULTS FOR eDNA 18S ===\n")
print(mrm_result_edna)

# Extract key results
env_coef_edna <- mrm_result_edna$coef[2,1]
geo_coef_edna <- mrm_result_edna$coef[3,1]
env_p_edna <- mrm_result_edna$coef[2,2]
geo_p_edna <- mrm_result_edna$coef[3,2]
model_r2_edna <- mrm_result_edna$r.squared[1]
model_p_edna <- mrm_result_edna$r.squared[2]

cat("\nKey Findings:\n")
cat(sprintf("Model R²: %.3f (p = %.3f)\n", model_r2_edna, model_p_edna))
cat(sprintf("Environmental coefficient: %.3f (p = %.3f) %s\n", 
            env_coef_edna, env_p_edna, ifelse(env_p_edna < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
cat(sprintf("Geographic coefficient: %.3f (p = %.3f) %s\n", 
            geo_coef_edna, geo_p_edna, ifelse(geo_p_edna < 0.05, "*", "ns")))

```


# Extract Results
```{r Extract Results}

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("EXTRACTING AND SAVING RESULTS - eDNA 18S\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

# Mantel results
mantel_results_edna <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("eDNA_18S", "eDNA_18S"),
  Distance_Type = c("Environmental", "Geographic"),
  Mantel_r = c(mantel_env_edna$statistic, mantel_geo_edna$statistic),
  P_value = c(mantel_env_edna$signif, mantel_geo_edna$signif),
  Significance = c(
    ifelse(mantel_env_edna$signif < 0.001, "***",
           ifelse(mantel_env_edna$signif < 0.01, "**",
                  ifelse(mantel_env_edna$signif < 0.05, "*", "ns"))),
    ifelse(mantel_geo_edna$signif < 0.001, "***",
           ifelse(mantel_geo_edna$signif < 0.01, "**",
                  ifelse(mantel_geo_edna$signif < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Partial Mantel results
partial_mantel_results_edna <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("eDNA_18S", "eDNA_18S"),
  Test_Variable = c("Environmental_controlling_Geographic", "Geographic_controlling_Environmental"),
  Partial_Mantel_r = c(partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$statistic, partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$statistic),
  P_value = c(partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$signif, partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$signif),
  Significance = c(
    ifelse(partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$signif < 0.001, "***",
           ifelse(partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$signif < 0.01, "**",
                  ifelse(partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$signif < 0.05, "*", "ns"))),
    ifelse(partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$signif < 0.001, "***",
           ifelse(partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$signif < 0.01, "**",
                  ifelse(partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$signif < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# MRM results
mrm_results_edna <- data.frame(
  Dataset = c("eDNA_18S", "eDNA_18S"),
  Variable = c("Environmental_Distance", "Geographic_Distance"),
  Beta_Coefficient = c(env_coef_edna, geo_coef_edna),
  P_value = c(env_p_edna, geo_p_edna),
  Model_R2 = c(model_r2_edna, model_r2_edna),
  Model_P_value = c(model_p_edna, model_p_edna),
  Significance = c(
    ifelse(env_p_edna < 0.001, "***",
           ifelse(env_p_edna < 0.01, "**",
                  ifelse(env_p_edna < 0.05, "*", "ns"))),
    ifelse(geo_p_edna < 0.001, "***",
           ifelse(geo_p_edna < 0.01, "**",
                  ifelse(geo_p_edna < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

#======= SAVE RESULTS =======

write.csv(mantel_results_edna, file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_18S_Mantel_Results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(partial_mantel_results_edna, file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_18S_Partial_Mantel_Results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(mrm_results_edna, file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_18S_MRM_Results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```


# Create Data Visualizations
```{r Create Data Visualizations}

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("CREATING VISUALIZATIONS - eDNA 18S\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

# Prepare visualization data
viz_data_edna <- data.frame(
  BC_Distance = bc_dist_vec_edna,
  Env_Distance = env_dist_vec_edna,
  Geo_Distance = geo_dist_vec_edna,
  Dataset = "eDNA 18S"
)

# 1. Environmental effects plot
p1_edna <- ggplot(viz_data_edna, aes(x = Env_Distance, y = BC_Distance)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8, color = "#408BA8") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#408BA8", fill = "#408BA8") +
  labs(x = "Environmental Distance (log-transformed)",
       y = "Community Dissimilarity (Bray-Curtis)",
       title = "eDNA 18S: Environmental Filtering",
       subtitle = paste0("Mantel r = ", round(mantel_env_edna$statistic, 3), 
                        ", p = ", round(mantel_env_edna$signif, 3))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10))

# 2. Geographic effects plot
p2_edna <- ggplot(viz_data_edna, aes(x = Geo_Distance, y = BC_Distance)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.8, color = "#812A5A") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#812A5A", fill = "#812A5A") +
  labs(x = "Geographic Distance (log-transformed km)",
       y = "Community Dissimilarity (Bray-Curtis)",
       title = "eDNA 18S: Dispersal Limitation", 
       subtitle = paste0("Mantel r = ", round(mantel_geo_edna$statistic, 3),
                        ", p = ", round(mantel_geo_edna$signif, 3))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10))

# 3. MRM coefficients plot
effect_sizes_edna <- data.frame(
  Variable = c("Environmental", "Geographic"),
  Coefficient = c(env_coef_edna, geo_coef_edna),
  P_value = c(env_p_edna, geo_p_edna),
  Significance = c(
    ifelse(env_p_edna < 0.001, "***",
           ifelse(env_p_edna < 0.01, "**",
                  ifelse(env_p_edna < 0.05, "*", "ns"))),
    ifelse(geo_p_edna < 0.001, "***",
           ifelse(geo_p_edna < 0.01, "**",
                  ifelse(geo_p_edna < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
  )
)

p3_edna <- ggplot(effect_sizes_edna, aes(x = Variable, y = Coefficient, fill = Variable)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Significance), 
            vjust = ifelse(effect_sizes_edna$Coefficient >= 0, -0.5, 1.2), 
            size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  labs(x = "Distance Type",
       y = "Standardized Coefficient (β)",
       title = "eDNA 18S: MRM Effect Sizes",
       subtitle = paste0("Model R² = ", round(model_r2_edna, 3), ", p = ", round(model_p_edna, 3))) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Environmental" = "#408BA8", "Geographic" = "#812A5A")) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.7)

# 4. Summary comparison plot
summary_data_edna <- data.frame(
  Test = c("Environmental\nMantel", "Geographic\nMantel", "Partial Env\n(|Geography)", "Partial Geo\n(|Environment)"),
  Correlation = c(mantel_env_edna$statistic, mantel_geo_edna$statistic,
                  partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$statistic, partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$statistic),
  P_value = c(mantel_env_edna$signif, mantel_geo_edna$signif,
              partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$signif, partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$signif),
  Significant = factor(c(mantel_env_edna$signif < 0.05, mantel_geo_edna$signif < 0.05,
                        partial_mantel_env_geo_edna$signif < 0.05, partial_mantel_geo_env_edna$signif < 0.05))
)

p4_edna <- ggplot(summary_data_edna, aes(x = reorder(Test, abs(Correlation)), y = Correlation, fill = Significant)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0("p=", round(P_value, 3))), 
            hjust = ifelse(summary_data_edna$Correlation >= 0, -0.1, 1.1), 
            size = 3) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Analysis Type",
       y = "Correlation Coefficient",
       title = "eDNA 18S: Analysis Summary",
       fill = "Significant\n(p < 0.05)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#408BA8", "FALSE" = "grey70"))

#======= COMBINE PLOTS =======
combined_plot_edna <- grid.arrange(p1_edna, p2_edna, p3_edna, p4_edna, 
                                   layout_matrix = rbind(c(1,2), c(3,4)))

#======= SAVE PLOTS =======
ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_18S_Distance_Relationships.pdf"), 
       combined_plot_edna, width = 16, height = 11, dpi = 300)

ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_18S_Distance_Relationships.png"), 
       combined_plot_edna, width = 16, height = 11, dpi = 300)

cat("✓ Visualizations saved to Output_eDNA directory\n")
cat("  - eDNA_18S_Distance_Relationships.pdf\n")
cat("  - eDNA_18S_Distance_Relationships.png\n")

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("ANALYSIS COMPLETE!\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

```



# =======================================
# ------ Relative Abundance -------------
# =======================================
eDNA 18S RELATIVE ABUNDANCE PLOTS - TOP 20 PHYLA
Adapted from ARMS code for eDNA dataset (no fractions)
```{r}
#library(ggplot2)
#library(dplyr)

# Site ordering by turbidity (provided)
turb_ordered_sites <- c("McGrail_deep","EFGB_shallow", "Alderdice_shallow", 
                        "Bright_shallow", "Diaphus_deep2", "EFGB_deep", 
                        "Diaphus_deep1", "Bright_deep2", "Bright_deep1", 
                        "Alderdice_deep1", "Stetson_shallow", "Alderdice_deep2")

# Phylum color mapping (provided)
phylum_color_map <- c("Annelida" = "#60714E",
                      "Chaetognatha" = "#015951",
                      "Arthropoda" = "#3B8C6E",
                      "Sipuncula" = "#B0DBBB",
                      "Bryozoa" = "#5BBFB0",
                      "Brachiopoda" = "#9DC9D3",
                      "Chordata" = "#408BA8",
                      "Rotifera" = "#0D3D59",
                      "Cnidaria" = "#594A9E",
                      "Echinodermata" = "#A8B1D6",
                      "Entoprocta" = "#812A5A",
                      "Mollusca" = "#F69B9C",
                      "Rhodophyta" = "#F16363",
                      "Nematoda" = "#C74238",
                      "Nemertea" = "#FEE14A",
                      "Platyhelminthes" = "#FEBC2C",
                      "Phoronida" = "#C47B51",
                      "Porifera" = "#F68C30",
                      "Ctenophora" = "#F05522",
                      "Gastrotricha" = "#FDCA9D",
                      "Unknown" = "grey40")

# Create output directory
if(!dir.exists("./Taxa_Plots_eDNA")) {
  dir.create("./Taxa_Plots_eDNA")
}

cat("=== eDNA 18S RELATIVE ABUNDANCE ANALYSIS ===\n")

# =============== EXTRACT DATA FROM PHYLOSEQ OBJECT ========================

# Extract matrices and metadata from phyloseq object
otu_matrix_edna <- as(otu_table(physeq_eDNA_rarefied), "matrix")
tax_matrix_edna <- as(tax_table(physeq_eDNA_rarefied), "matrix")
sample_data_df_edna <- metadata_edna  # Use our cleaned metadata

# Ensure samples are columns in OTU matrix
if(taxa_are_rows(physeq_eDNA_rarefied)) {
  # OTU matrix is ASVs x Samples, which is what we want
  cat("OTU matrix orientation: ASVs as rows, samples as columns\n")
} else {
  # Need to transpose
  otu_matrix_edna <- t(otu_matrix_edna)
  cat("Transposed OTU matrix: ASVs as rows, samples as columns\n")
}

cat("OTU matrix dimensions:", dim(otu_matrix_edna), "(ASVs x Samples)\n")
cat("Taxonomy matrix dimensions:", dim(tax_matrix_edna), "(ASVs x Tax levels)\n")
cat("Sample metadata dimensions:", dim(sample_data_df_edna), "\n")

# ===============  CALCULATE PHYLUM-LEVEL ABUNDANCES =====================

# Create sample metadata dataframe (simplified for eDNA - no fractions)
sample_metadata_edna <- data.frame(
  Sample = rownames(sample_data_df_edna),
  sitelocality = as.character(sample_data_df_edna$sitelocality),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

cat("Sample metadata created for", nrow(sample_metadata_edna), "samples\n")

# Group ASVs by phylum
phylum_groups_edna <- split(rownames(tax_matrix_edna), tax_matrix_edna[, "phylum"])
cat("Found", length(phylum_groups_edna), "phyla\n")

# Calculate phylum-level abundances
phylum_abundances_edna <- lapply(names(phylum_groups_edna), function(phylum) {
  asvs <- phylum_groups_edna[[phylum]]
  if (length(asvs) == 1) {
    # If only one ASV in phylum
    abundance <- otu_matrix_edna[asvs, , drop = FALSE]
  } else {
    # Sum abundances of all ASVs in the phylum
    abundance <- colSums(otu_matrix_edna[asvs, , drop = FALSE])
  }
  data.frame(
    Sample = colnames(otu_matrix_edna),
    phylum = phylum,
    Abundance = as.numeric(abundance),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

# Combine all phylum abundances
phylum_abundance_df_edna <- do.call(rbind, phylum_abundances_edna)

# Merge with sample metadata
phylum_abundance_df_edna <- merge(phylum_abundance_df_edna, sample_metadata_edna, by = "Sample")

# ===============  IDENTIFY TOP 20 PHYLA ==========================

# Calculate total abundance by phylum
phylum_totals_edna <- aggregate(Abundance ~ phylum, data = phylum_abundance_df_edna, sum)
phylum_totals_edna <- phylum_totals_edna[order(phylum_totals_edna$Abundance, decreasing = TRUE), ]

# Get top 20 phyla
top_20_phyla_edna <- phylum_totals_edna$phylum[1:20]
cat("Top 20 phyla by total abundance:\n")
print(phylum_totals_edna[1:20, ])

# Filter to only include top 20 phyla
phylum_abundance_df_filtered_edna <- phylum_abundance_df_edna[phylum_abundance_df_edna$phylum %in% top_20_phyla_edna, ]

# Calculate relative abundance
relative_data_edna <- phylum_abundance_df_filtered_edna %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  mutate(Relative_Abundance = Abundance / sum(Abundance) * 100) %>%
  ungroup()

# Order sites according to turbidity ordering
relative_data_edna$sitelocality <- factor(relative_data_edna$sitelocality, levels = turb_ordered_sites)

cat("Relative abundance data prepared for", nrow(relative_data_edna), "observations\n")

# ============ PLOT 1: RELATIVE ABUNDANCE BY SAMPLE ======================

cat("Creating sample-level relative abundance plot...\n")

p_sample_edna <- ggplot(relative_data_edna, aes(x = Sample, y = Relative_Abundance, fill = phylum)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", 
           color = "black", size = 0.2, width = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_color_map) +
  labs(y = "Relative abundance (%)", 
       x = "Sample", 
       title = "eDNA 18S: Relative Abundance of Top 20 Phyla by Sample",
       fill = "Phylum") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 8, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(p_sample_edna)

# Save sample-level plot
ggsave("./Taxa_Plots_eDNA/eDNA_18S_Top20_Phyla_RelativeAbundance_bySample.pdf", 
       p_sample_edna, width = 16, height = 8)

# ============  PLOT 2: RELATIVE ABUNDANCE BY SITE LOCALITY (AGGREGATED) =================

# Aggregate data by site locality
site_aggregated_edna <- relative_data_edna %>%
  group_by(sitelocality, phylum) %>%
  summarise(Relative_Abundance = mean(Relative_Abundance, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  ungroup()

# Ensure site order is maintained
site_aggregated_edna$sitelocality <- factor(site_aggregated_edna$sitelocality, levels = turb_ordered_sites)

# Create site-level plot
p_site_edna <- ggplot(site_aggregated_edna, aes(x = sitelocality, y = Relative_Abundance, fill = phylum)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack",
           color = "black", linewidth = 0.4, width = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_color_map, name = "Phylum", drop = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 100), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  labs(y = "Relative Abundance (%)", 
       x = "Site Locality (ordered by turbidity: clear → turbid)", 
       title = "eDNA 18S Phylum Community by Site",
       subtitle = "Mean relative abundance aggregated by site locality") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 17, color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14, color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 17, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "cm")
  )

print(p_site_edna)

# Save site-level plot
ggsave("./Taxa_Plots_eDNA/eDNA_18S_Top20_Phyla_RelativeAbundance_bySite.pdf", 
       p_site_edna, width = 6.8, height = 10)

# =============  PLOT 3: COMBINED PLOT SHOWING SAMPLE NAMES GROUPED BY SITE ================

cat("Creating combined plot with samples grouped by site...\n")

# Create a plot where samples are grouped by site but still shown individually
p_combined_edna <- ggplot(relative_data_edna, aes(x = Sample, y = Relative_Abundance, fill = phylum)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", 
           color = "black", size = 0.1, width = 0.9) +
  facet_grid(~ sitelocality, scales = "free_x", space = "free_x") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_color_map) +
  labs(y = "Relative abundance (%)", 
       x = "Sample (grouped by site)", 
       title = "eDNA 18S: Relative Abundance of Top 20 Phyla by Sample and Site",
       fill = "Phylum") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_blank(),  # Hide individual sample names for clarity
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 14, color = "black"),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold", angle = 45),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(0.2, "cm"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5)
  )

print(p_combined_edna)

# Save combined plot
ggsave("./Taxa_Plots_eDNA/eDNA_18S_Top20_Phyla_RelativeAbundance_Combined.pdf", 
       p_combined_edna, width = 18, height = 8)

# ===========  SUMMARY STATISTICS ========================

cat("\n=== eDNA RELATIVE ABUNDANCE SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Total samples analyzed:", length(unique(relative_data_edna$Sample)), "\n")
cat("Total sites:", length(unique(relative_data_edna$sitelocality)), "\n")
cat("Top 20 phyla represented:", length(unique(relative_data_edna$phylum)), "\n")

# Calculate coverage of top 20 phyla
coverage_by_sample_edna <- relative_data_edna %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  summarise(Coverage = sum(Relative_Abundance), .groups = 'drop')

cat("Mean coverage by top 20 phyla:", round(mean(coverage_by_sample_edna$Coverage), 1), "%\n")
cat("Range of coverage:", round(min(coverage_by_sample_edna$Coverage), 1), "-", 
    round(max(coverage_by_sample_edna$Coverage), 1), "%\n")

# Show most abundant phyla
cat("\nMost abundant phyla overall:\n")
overall_abundance_edna <- relative_data_edna %>%
  group_by(phylum) %>%
  summarise(Mean_Abundance = mean(Relative_Abundance), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  arrange(desc(Mean_Abundance))

print(head(overall_abundance_edna, 10)) 
```

# Export .CSV of mapped unique families
```{r}
tax_subset_eDNA <- tax_df[, c("phylum", "class", "order", "family")]
unique_families_eDNA <- unique(tax_subset_eDNA)

# Remove any rows where Family is NA
unique_families_eDNA <- unique_families_eDNA[order(unique_families_eDNA$phylum, 
                                          unique_families_eDNA$class,
                                          unique_families_eDNA$order,
                                          unique_families_eDNA$family), ]


unique_families_clean_eDNA <- unique_families_eDNA[!grepl("[^[:alnum:]]$", unique_families_eDNA$family), ]

# Remove taxonomic errors
unique_families_metazoa_only_eDNA <- unique_families_clean_eDNA[
  !unique_families_clean_eDNA$family %in% taxonomic_errors, ]

write.csv(unique_families_metazoa_only_eDNA, 
          file = "./unique_families_eDNA_clean.csv", 
          row.names = FALSE)
```

# =======================================
# ------------ ANCOM-BC2 ----------------
# =======================================

PURPOSE:
Identifies taxa that are significantly associated with environmentalgradients and site-specific effects in pelagic eDNA communities using
compositionally-aware differential abundance testing (ANCOMBC2).

WHAT THIS CODE DOES:
1. Site Effects Analysis - Identifies taxa that vary significantly across
    the 12 sampling sites, capturing unmeasured local environmental factors
    and fine-scale habitat heterogeneity
 
2. Environmental Effects Analysis - Tests which taxa are associated with:
    - Depth (primary light/pressure gradient)
    - Turbidity (water clarity/particle suspension)
    - Productivity (bottom-up energetic control)
    Tests both basic model (depth + turbidity) and full model (+ productivity)
 
3. Visualization - Creates barplots showing relative abundance of significant
    taxa across sites, ordered by environmental gradients with color-coded
    environmental values
 
REQUIRED INPUTS:
 - physeq_eDNA18S: phyloseq object containing:
   * ASV abundance table (otu_table)
   * Taxonomic assignments with 'phylum' and 'family' ranks (tax_table)
   * Sample metadata (sample_data) with columns:
     - sitelocality: site names (categorical)
     - Depth: depth in meters (numeric)
     - Visibility_std_rank: standardized turbidity rank (numeric)
     - pp_mean: primary productivity (numeric)
 
 - metadata_edna: data frame with matching sample metadata (optional, for plotting)

OUTPUTS:
Directory structure: ./ANCOMBC_eDNA/
 
A. SITE EFFECTS (CSV files):
    - site_phylum_global_results_eDNA.csv: Global test results at phylum level
    - site_phylum_pairwise_results_eDNA.csv: Pairwise site comparisons (phyla)
    - site_family_global_results_eDNA.csv: Global test results at family level
    - site_family_pairwise_results_eDNA.csv: Pairwise site comparisons (families)
 
B. ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS (CSV files):
    - environmental_phylum_basic_full_eDNA.csv: Depth + turbidity model (phyla)
    - environmental_phylum_full_model_eDNA.csv: + productivity model (phyla)
    - environmental_family_basic_full_eDNA.csv: Depth + turbidity model (families)
    - environmental_family_full_model_eDNA.csv: + productivity model (families)
    - all_environmental_significant_results_eDNA.csv: Combined significant results
    - environmental_analysis_summary_eDNA.csv: Summary statistics table
 
C. VISUALIZATIONS (PDF files):
    - eDNA_Depth_Phyla_Effects.pdf: Barplots of depth-associated phyla
    - eDNA_Turbidity_Phyla_Effects.pdf: Barplots of turbidity-associated phyla
    - eDNA_Productivity_Phyla_Effects.pdf: Barplots of productivity-associated phyla
 
Each significant result includes:
 - Taxon name
 - Log-fold change (effect size and direction)
 - P-value and Q-value (FDR-adjusted)
 - Robust flag (passed structural zero sensitivity analysis)
 - Direction of association (increases/decreases with environmental variable)
 
INTERPRETATION:
 - Significant taxa = compositionally associated with environmental gradients
 - Positive LFC = taxon increases in abundance with increasing environmental value
 - Negative LFC = taxon decreases in abundance with increasing environmental value
 - Robust effects (passed_ss = TRUE) are more reliable and less sensitive to assumptions about structural zeros
 
COMPARISON WITH ARMS:
Use these results alongside ARMS ANCOMBC2 analyses to compare:
 - Which taxa respond to environment in pelagic vs benthic communities
 - Whether dispersal mode (pelagic vs sessile) affects environmental sensitivity
 - Taxon-specific habitat associations and niche breadth
```{r, ANCOMBC setup}

library(ANCOMBC)
library(phyloseq)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)
library(viridis)

# Set preferences
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::select)
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::filter)
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::mutate)
conflicts_prefer(dplyr::summarise)

# Create output directory
output_dir <- "./ANCOMBC_eDNA"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}
```

# Diagnostics for setting prv_cut value
```{r}
# ============ eDNA PREVALENCE ANALYSIS - DIAGNOSTIC FOR prv_cut DECISION ===================================

library(phyloseq)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Extract OTU table
otu_table_edna <- as(otu_table(physeq_eDNA18S), "matrix")

# If taxa are rows, transpose
if(taxa_are_rows(physeq_eDNA18S)) {
  otu_table_edna <- t(otu_table_edna)
}

# Calculate prevalence (number of samples where each taxon appears)
prevalence_counts <- colSums(otu_table_edna > 0)

# Get total abundance
total_abundance <- colSums(otu_table_edna)

# Create prevalence data frame
prevalence_df <- data.frame(
  ASV = names(prevalence_counts),
  Prevalence = prevalence_counts,
  TotalAbundance = total_abundance,
  Prevalence_Proportion = prevalence_counts / nsamples(physeq_eDNA18S)
)

# Add taxonomy
tax_df <- as.data.frame(tax_table(physeq_eDNA18S))
prevalence_df <- cbind(prevalence_df, tax_df[match(prevalence_df$ASV, rownames(tax_df)), ])

# Overall summary
cat("=== eDNA PREVALENCE SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Total samples:", nsamples(physeq_eDNA18S), "\n")
cat("Total ASVs:", nrow(prevalence_df), "\n\n")

cat("ASVs retained at different prevalence cutoffs:\n")
cat("prv_cut = 0.05 (≥2 samples):", sum(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.05), 
    "(", round(sum(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.05)/nrow(prevalence_df)*100, 1), "%)\n")
cat("prv_cut = 0.10 (≥4 samples):", sum(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.10), 
    "(", round(sum(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.10)/nrow(prevalence_df)*100, 1), "%)\n")
cat("prv_cut = 0.20 (≥7 samples):", sum(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.20), 
    "(", round(sum(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.20)/nrow(prevalence_df)*100, 1), "%)\n")
cat("prv_cut = 0.30 (≥10 samples):", sum(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.30), 
    "(", round(sum(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.30)/nrow(prevalence_df)*100, 1), "%)\n")

# Summarize by phylum
prevalence_by_phylum <- prevalence_df %>%
  group_by(phylum) %>%
  summarise(
    N_ASVs = n(),
    Mean_Prevalence = mean(Prevalence_Proportion),
    Median_Prevalence = median(Prevalence_Proportion),
    N_Above_10pct = sum(Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.1),
    N_Above_20pct = sum(Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.2),
    N_Above_30pct = sum(Prevalence_Proportion >= 0.3),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(N_ASVs))

cat("\n=== PREVALENCE BY PHYLUM ===\n")
print(prevalence_by_phylum, n = 20)

# Visualize overall prevalence distribution
p1 <- ggplot(prevalence_df, aes(x = Prevalence_Proportion)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3), 
             linetype = "dashed", color = c("orange", "red", "darkred"), 
             linewidth = 1) +
  annotate("text", x = 0.1, y = Inf, label = "10%", vjust = 1.5, color = "orange", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.2, y = Inf, label = "20%", vjust = 1.5, color = "red", fontface = "bold") +
  annotate("text", x = 0.3, y = Inf, label = "30%", vjust = 1.5, color = "darkred", fontface = "bold") +
  labs(title = "eDNA ASV Prevalence Distribution",
       subtitle = paste0("Total ASVs = ", nrow(prevalence_df), 
                        " across ", nsamples(physeq_eDNA18S), " samples"),
       x = "Proportion of Samples",
       y = "Number of ASVs") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))

print(p1)

# Visualize prevalence vs abundance
p2 <- ggplot(prevalence_df, aes(x = Prevalence_Proportion, y = log10(TotalAbundance + 1))) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3), 
             linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(title = "eDNA: Prevalence vs Total Abundance",
       subtitle = "Rare taxa (low prevalence) often have low total abundance",
       x = "Prevalence (Proportion of Samples)",
       y = "Log10(Total Abundance + 1)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))

print(p2)

# Prevalence distribution by major phyla
major_phyla <- prevalence_by_phylum %>%
  filter(N_ASVs >= 10) %>%
  pull(phylum)

if(length(major_phyla) > 0) {
  p3 <- prevalence_df %>%
    filter(phylum %in% major_phyla) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = Prevalence_Proportion, fill = phylum)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(0.1, 0.2, 0.3), 
               linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
    facet_wrap(~phylum, scales = "free_y") +
    labs(title = "Prevalence Distribution by Major Phyla",
         x = "Prevalence (Proportion of Samples)",
         y = "Density") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(legend.position = "none",
          plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))
  
  print(p3)
}

# Summary statistics
cat("\n=== PREVALENCE STATISTICS ===\n")
cat("Median prevalence:", round(median(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion), 3), "\n")
cat("Mean prevalence:", round(mean(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion), 3), "\n")
cat("Prevalence range:", round(min(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion), 3), "-", 
    round(max(prevalence_df$Prevalence_Proportion), 3), "\n")

# Identify highly prevalent taxa (present in >50% of samples)
highly_prevalent <- prevalence_df %>%
  filter(Prevalence_Proportion > 0.5) %>%
  arrange(desc(Prevalence_Proportion))

cat("\n=== HIGHLY PREVALENT TAXA (>50% of samples) ===\n")
cat("Number of ASVs:", nrow(highly_prevalent), "\n")
if(nrow(highly_prevalent) > 0) {
  print(highly_prevalent %>% 
        select(phylum, class, order, family, Prevalence, Prevalence_Proportion, TotalAbundance) %>%
        head(20))
}

# Save the prevalence data for reference
write.csv(prevalence_df, "./ANCOMBC_eDNA/eDNA_ASV_prevalence_analysis.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(prevalence_by_phylum, "./ANCOMBC_eDNA/eDNA_prevalence_by_phylum.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n✓ Prevalence analysis complete\n")
cat("✓ Files saved to ./ANCOMBC_eDNA/\n")
```



```{r ANCOMBC2 on site, depth, turb, and productivity}
# ===========  1. SITE EFFECTS ANALYSIS  ==============
# Note: With only 2-3 samples per site, ANCOMBC2 variance estimation
# is unstable. We run global tests only (no pairwise comparisons) to
# identify which taxa show overall site-level variation.

cat("\n=== RUNNING SITE EFFECTS ANALYSIS ===\n")
cat("Note: Small sample size per site (<5) limits power for site effects\n")
cat("      Focus on environmental effects analysis for robust results\n\n")

# Phylum level - site effects (global test only)
site_P_ancombc_edna <- ancombc2(
  data = physeq_eDNA18S,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "sitelocality",
  global = TRUE,
  pairwise = FALSE,  # Skip pairwise due to small n
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.2,
  group = "sitelocality",
  pseudo_sens = FALSE  # Skip sensitivity analysis for site effects
)

# Family level - site effects (global test only)
site_F_ancombc_edna <- ancombc2(
  data = physeq_eDNA18S,
  tax_level = "family",
  fix_formula = "sitelocality",
  global = TRUE,
  pairwise = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.2,
  group = "sitelocality",
  pseudo_sens = FALSE
)

# Extract results
site_phylum_results_edna <- site_P_ancombc_edna$res_global
site_family_results_edna <- site_F_ancombc_edna$res_global

# Add analysis metadata
site_phylum_results_edna$Analysis <- "eDNA_Site_Effects"
site_phylum_results_edna$Taxonomic_Level <- "Phylum"
site_family_results_edna$Analysis <- "eDNA_Site_Effects"
site_family_results_edna$Taxonomic_Level <- "Family"

# Export site results
write.csv(site_phylum_results_edna, 
          file.path(output_dir, "site_phylum_global_results_eDNA.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(site_family_results_edna, 
          file.path(output_dir, "site_family_global_results_eDNA.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("✓ Site effects analysis completed\n")
cat("  - Phylum level:", nrow(site_phylum_results_edna), "taxa tested\n")
cat("  - Family level:", nrow(site_family_results_edna), "taxa tested\n")
cat("  - Significant phyla:", sum(site_phylum_results_edna$diff_abn, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("  - Significant families:", sum(site_family_results_edna$diff_abn, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")

# ============  2. ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS ANALYSIS ===============
# Taxa associated with depth, turbidity, and productivity
# Uses continuous predictors across all 33 samples = better power

cat("\n=== RUNNING ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS ANALYSIS ===\n")
cat("Using prv_cut = 0.2 (taxa present in ≥7 samples, 21%)\n\n")

# Phylum level - environmental effects (depth + turbidity)
env_P_basic_edna <- ancombc2(
  data = physeq_eDNA18S,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "Depth + Visibility_std_rank",
  rand_formula = NULL,
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.2,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# Phylum level - full environmental model (+ productivity)
env_P_full_edna <- ancombc2(
  data = physeq_eDNA18S,
  tax_level = "phylum",
  fix_formula = "Depth + Visibility_std_rank + pp_mean",
  rand_formula = NULL,
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.2,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# Family level - environmental effects (depth + turbidity)
env_F_basic_edna <- ancombc2(
  data = physeq_eDNA18S,
  tax_level = "family",
  fix_formula = "Depth + Visibility_std_rank",
  rand_formula = NULL,
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.2,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

# Family level - full environmental model (+ productivity)
env_F_full_edna <- ancombc2(
  data = physeq_eDNA18S,
  tax_level = "family",
  fix_formula = "Depth + Visibility_std_rank + pp_mean",
  rand_formula = NULL,
  global = FALSE,
  p_adj_method = "BH",
  prv_cut = 0.2,
  pseudo_sens = TRUE
)

cat("✓ Environmental effects analyses completed\n")

# ============  3. EXTRACT AND FORMAT ENVIRONMENTAL RESULTS ==============

cat("\n=== EXTRACTING ENVIRONMENTAL RESULTS ===\n")

# Simplified extraction function for clean column names
extract_env_significant_edna <- function(ancombc_result, model_name, tax_level) {
  
  results <- ancombc_result$res
  all_results <- data.frame()
  
  # === DEPTH EFFECTS ===
  if("diff_Depth" %in% colnames(results)) {
    n_sig <- sum(results$diff_Depth, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    if(n_sig > 0) {
      depth_sig <- which(results$diff_Depth == TRUE)
      
      depth_data <- data.frame(
        Model = model_name,
        Taxonomic_Level = tax_level,
        Environmental_Variable = "Depth",
        Taxon = results$taxon[depth_sig],
        Log_Fold_Change = round(results$lfc_Depth[depth_sig], 4),
        SE = round(results$se_Depth[depth_sig], 4),
        P_value = results$p_Depth[depth_sig],
        Q_value = results$q_Depth[depth_sig],
        Robust = results$passed_ss_Depth[depth_sig],
        Direction = ifelse(results$lfc_Depth[depth_sig] > 0, "Increases", "Decreases"),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      all_results <- rbind(all_results, depth_data)
      cat("  ✓ Extracted", n_sig, "significant depth effects\n")
    }
  }
  
  # === TURBIDITY EFFECTS ===
  if("diff_Visibility_std_rank" %in% colnames(results)) {
    n_sig <- sum(results$diff_Visibility_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    if(n_sig > 0) {
      turb_sig <- which(results$diff_Visibility_std_rank == TRUE)
      
      turb_data <- data.frame(
        Model = model_name,
        Taxonomic_Level = tax_level,
        Environmental_Variable = "Turbidity",
        Taxon = results$taxon[turb_sig],
        Log_Fold_Change = round(results$lfc_Visibility_std_rank[turb_sig], 4),
        SE = round(results$se_Visibility_std_rank[turb_sig], 4),
        P_value = results$p_Visibility_std_rank[turb_sig],
        Q_value = results$q_Visibility_std_rank[turb_sig],
        Robust = results$passed_ss_Visibility_std_rank[turb_sig],
        Direction = ifelse(results$lfc_Visibility_std_rank[turb_sig] > 0, "Increases", "Decreases"),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      all_results <- rbind(all_results, turb_data)
      cat("  ✓ Extracted", n_sig, "significant turbidity effects\n")
    }
  }
  
  # === PRODUCTIVITY EFFECTS ===
  if("diff_pp_mean" %in% colnames(results)) {
    n_sig <- sum(results$diff_pp_mean, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    if(n_sig > 0) {
      prod_sig <- which(results$diff_pp_mean == TRUE)
      
      prod_data <- data.frame(
        Model = model_name,
        Taxonomic_Level = tax_level,
        Environmental_Variable = "Productivity",
        Taxon = results$taxon[prod_sig],
        Log_Fold_Change = round(results$lfc_pp_mean[prod_sig], 4),
        SE = round(results$se_pp_mean[prod_sig], 4),
        P_value = results$p_pp_mean[prod_sig],
        Q_value = results$q_pp_mean[prod_sig],
        Robust = results$passed_ss_pp_mean[prod_sig],
        Direction = ifelse(results$lfc_pp_mean[prod_sig] > 0, "Increases", "Decreases"),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      all_results <- rbind(all_results, prod_data)
      cat("  ✓ Extracted", n_sig, "significant productivity effects\n")
    }
  }
  
  if(nrow(all_results) == 0) {
    cat("  ⚠ No significant effects found for", model_name, "-", tax_level, "\n")
  }
  
  return(all_results)
}

# Extract significant results for all models
cat("\nBasic Environmental Model - Phylum:\n")
sig_env_P_basic_edna <- extract_env_significant_edna(env_P_basic_edna, "Basic_Environmental", "Phylum")

cat("\nFull Environmental Model - Phylum:\n")
sig_env_P_full_edna <- extract_env_significant_edna(env_P_full_edna, "Full_Environmental", "Phylum")

cat("\nBasic Environmental Model - Family:\n")
sig_env_F_basic_edna <- extract_env_significant_edna(env_F_basic_edna, "Basic_Environmental", "Family")

cat("\nFull Environmental Model - Family:\n")
sig_env_F_full_edna <- extract_env_significant_edna(env_F_full_edna, "Full_Environmental", "Family")

# Combine all significant results
all_env_significant_edna <- rbind(
  sig_env_P_basic_edna,
  sig_env_P_full_edna,
  sig_env_F_basic_edna,
  sig_env_F_full_edna
)

# Export full results (all taxa, not just significant)
write.csv(env_P_basic_edna$res, 
          file.path(output_dir, "environmental_phylum_basic_full_eDNA.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(env_P_full_edna$res, 
          file.path(output_dir, "environmental_phylum_full_model_eDNA.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(env_F_basic_edna$res, 
          file.path(output_dir, "environmental_family_basic_full_eDNA.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(env_F_full_edna$res, 
          file.path(output_dir, "environmental_family_full_model_eDNA.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)

# Export combined significant results
write.csv(all_env_significant_edna, 
          file.path(output_dir, "all_environmental_significant_results_eDNA.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)

# Create summary table
env_summary_edna <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Basic_Phylum", "Full_Phylum", "Basic_Family", "Full_Family"),
  Total_Taxa = c(
    nrow(env_P_basic_edna$res),
    nrow(env_P_full_edna$res),
    nrow(env_F_basic_edna$res),
    nrow(env_F_full_edna$res)
  ),
  Significant_Depth = c(
    sum(env_P_basic_edna$res$diff_Depth, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_P_full_edna$res$diff_Depth, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_F_basic_edna$res$diff_Depth, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_F_full_edna$res$diff_Depth, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  Significant_Turbidity = c(
    sum(env_P_basic_edna$res$diff_Visibility_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_P_full_edna$res$diff_Visibility_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_F_basic_edna$res$diff_Visibility_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    sum(env_F_full_edna$res$diff_Visibility_std_rank, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  Significant_Productivity = c(
    0,
    sum(env_P_full_edna$res$diff_pp_mean, na.rm = TRUE),
    0,
    sum(env_F_full_edna$res$diff_pp_mean, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)

write.csv(env_summary_edna, 
          file.path(output_dir, "environmental_analysis_summary_eDNA.csv"), 
          row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n=== ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS SUMMARY ===\n")
print(env_summary_edna)

cat("\n=== SIGNIFICANT RESULTS PREVIEW ===\n")
if(nrow(all_env_significant_edna) > 0) {
  print(head(all_env_significant_edna, 20))
  cat("\nTotal significant environmental effects:", nrow(all_env_significant_edna), "\n")
  cat("Robust effects:", sum(all_env_significant_edna$Robust), "\n")
  cat("\nBy environmental variable:\n")
  cat("  Depth:", sum(all_env_significant_edna$Environmental_Variable == "Depth"), "\n")
  cat("  Turbidity:", sum(all_env_significant_edna$Environmental_Variable == "Turbidity"), "\n")
  cat("  Productivity:", sum(all_env_significant_edna$Environmental_Variable == "Productivity"), "\n")
} else {
  cat("No significant results found\n")
}

# ==========  4. VISUALIZATION: ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS BARPLOTS ===================

cat("\n=== CREATING ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS VISUALIZATIONS ===\n")

# Function to create environmental barplots for eDNA
create_edna_env_barplots <- function(physeq_obj, significant_results, 
                                     env_variable = "Turbidity",
                                     model_name = "Basic",
                                     tax_level = "Phylum",
                                     y_margin = 0.2) {
  
  # Filter for the specific environmental variable, model, and taxonomic level
  env_data <- significant_results %>% 
    filter(Environmental_Variable == env_variable & 
           Model == paste0(model_name, "_Environmental") &
           Taxonomic_Level == tax_level)
  
  if(nrow(env_data) == 0) {
    message(paste("No significant", env_variable, "effects found for eDNA", model_name, tax_level))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Get taxa of interest
  taxa_of_interest <- env_data$Taxon
  
  # Transform phyloseq data
  physeq_rel <- transform_sample_counts(physeq_obj, function(x) x/sum(x))
  
  # Melt to long format
  plot_data <- psmelt(physeq_rel)
  
  # Filter by appropriate taxonomic level
  if(tax_level == "Phylum") {
    plot_data <- plot_data %>% filter(phylum %in% taxa_of_interest)
    tax_col <- "phylum"
  } else {
    plot_data <- plot_data %>% filter(family %in% taxa_of_interest)
    tax_col <- "family"
  }
  
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
    message("No data found for specified taxa")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Get correct environmental variable name
  if(env_variable == "Turbidity") {
    env_var_name <- "Visibility_std_rank"
  } else if(env_variable == "Depth") {
    env_var_name <- "Depth"
  } else if(env_variable == "Productivity") {
    env_var_name <- "pp_mean"
  }
  
  # Calculate summary by site
  summary_data <- plot_data %>%
    group_by(across(all_of(tax_col)), sitelocality) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      mean_abundance = mean(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE),
      se = sd(Abundance, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
      env_value = mean(.data[[env_var_name]], na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  # Create plots for each taxon
  taxon_plots <- list()
  
  for(taxon in taxa_of_interest) {
    taxon_data <- summary_data %>% filter(.data[[tax_col]] == taxon)
    taxon_effect <- env_data %>% filter(Taxon == taxon)
    
    direction <- ifelse(taxon_effect$Log_Fold_Change > 0, "↑", "↓")
    robust_status <- ifelse(taxon_effect$Robust, "**", "*")
    effect_label <- paste0(direction, " ", env_variable, " ", robust_status)
    
    plot_max_y <- max(taxon_data$mean_abundance + taxon_data$se, na.rm = TRUE) * (1 + y_margin)
    
    # Create barplot with appropriate color scale
    if(env_variable == "Turbidity") {
      p1 <- ggplot(taxon_data, aes(x = reorder(sitelocality, env_value), 
                                   y = mean_abundance, 
                                   fill = env_value)) +
        scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "cividis", name = "Turbidity\n(Std Rank)", direction = 1)
    } else if(env_variable == "Depth") {
      p1 <- ggplot(taxon_data, aes(x = reorder(sitelocality, env_value), 
                                   y = mean_abundance, 
                                   fill = env_value)) +
        scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", name = "Depth\n(meters)", direction = -1)
    } else {
      p1 <- ggplot(taxon_data, aes(x = reorder(sitelocality, env_value), 
                                   y = mean_abundance, 
                                   fill = env_value)) +
        scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "viridis", name = "Productivity", direction = 1)
    }
    
    p1 <- p1 +
      geom_col(color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
      geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_abundance - se, ymax = mean_abundance + se),
                    width = 0.4, linewidth = 0.5) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(taxon, " (eDNA ", tax_level, ")"),
        subtitle = paste0("Effect: ", effect_label, 
                        " (LFC = ", round(taxon_effect$Log_Fold_Change, 3), 
                        ", q = ", format(taxon_effect$Q_value, scientific = TRUE, digits = 2), ")"),
        x = paste0("Site (ordered by ", tolower(env_variable), ")"),
        y = "Relative Abundance"
      ) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
      ) +
      ylim(0, plot_max_y)
    
    taxon_plots[[taxon]] <- p1
  }
  
  if(length(taxon_plots) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # Arrange in grid
  n_cols <- min(3, length(taxon_plots))
  
  grid_plot <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(
    grobs = taxon_plots,
    ncol = n_cols,
    top = grid::textGrob(
      paste0("eDNA Environmental Effects: ", env_variable, " (", model_name, " ", tax_level, ")"),
      gp = grid::gpar(fontsize = 16, fontface = "bold")
    )
  )
  
  return(list(grid_plot = grid_plot, individual_plots = taxon_plots))
}

# Create plots for phylum-level results
if(nrow(sig_env_P_basic_edna) > 0) {
  
  # Turbidity effects - phylum
  if(any(sig_env_P_basic_edna$Environmental_Variable == "Turbidity")) {
    edna_turb_phylum_plots <- create_edna_env_barplots(
      physeq_obj = physeq_eDNA18S,
      significant_results = all_env_significant_edna,
      env_variable = "Turbidity",
      model_name = "Basic",
      tax_level = "Phylum",
      y_margin = 0.2
    )
    
    if(!is.null(edna_turb_phylum_plots)) {
      ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_Turbidity_Phyla_Effects.pdf"), 
             edna_turb_phylum_plots$grid_plot,
             width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 300)
      cat("✓ Saved: eDNA_Turbidity_Phyla_Effects.pdf\n")
    }
  }
  
  # Depth effects - phylum
  if(any(sig_env_P_basic_edna$Environmental_Variable == "Depth")) {
    edna_depth_phylum_plots <- create_edna_env_barplots(
      physeq_obj = physeq_eDNA18S,
      significant_results = all_env_significant_edna,
      env_variable = "Depth",
      model_name = "Basic",
      tax_level = "Phylum",
      y_margin = 0.2
    )
    
    if(!is.null(edna_depth_phylum_plots)) {
      ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_Depth_Phyla_Effects.pdf"), 
             edna_depth_phylum_plots$grid_plot,
             width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 300)
      cat("✓ Saved: eDNA_Depth_Phyla_Effects.pdf\n")
    }
  }
}

# Create plots for family-level results
if(nrow(sig_env_F_basic_edna) > 0) {
  
  # Turbidity effects - family (limit to top effects to avoid too many plots)
  turb_families <- sig_env_F_basic_edna %>%
    filter(Environmental_Variable == "Turbidity") %>%
    arrange(Q_value) %>%
    slice(1:min(12, n()))  # Top 12 most significant
  
  if(nrow(turb_families) > 0) {
    edna_turb_family_plots <- create_edna_env_barplots(
      physeq_obj = physeq_eDNA18S,
      significant_results = turb_families,
      env_variable = "Turbidity",
      model_name = "Basic",
      tax_level = "Family",
      y_margin = 0.2
    )
    
    if(!is.null(edna_turb_family_plots)) {
      ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_Turbidity_Family_Effects_Top12.pdf"), 
             edna_turb_family_plots$grid_plot,
             width = 18, height = 15, dpi = 300)
      cat("✓ Saved: eDNA_Turbidity_Family_Effects_Top12.pdf (top 12 most significant)\n")
    }
  }
  
  # Depth effects - family (limit to top effects)
  depth_families <- sig_env_F_basic_edna %>%
    filter(Environmental_Variable == "Depth") %>%
    arrange(Q_value) %>%
    slice(1:min(12, n()))
  
  if(nrow(depth_families) > 0) {
    edna_depth_family_plots <- create_edna_env_barplots(
      physeq_obj = physeq_eDNA18S,
      significant_results = depth_families,
      env_variable = "Depth",
      model_name = "Basic",
      tax_level = "Family",
      y_margin = 0.2
    )
    
    if(!is.null(edna_depth_family_plots)) {
      ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_Depth_Family_Effects_Top12.pdf"), 
             edna_depth_family_plots$grid_plot,
             width = 18, height = 15, dpi = 300)
      cat("✓ Saved: eDNA_Depth_Family_Effects_Top12.pdf (top 12 most significant)\n")
    }
  }
}

# Productivity effects (if significant)
if(nrow(sig_env_P_full_edna) > 0 || nrow(sig_env_F_full_edna) > 0) {
  
  prod_effects <- all_env_significant_edna %>%
    filter(Environmental_Variable == "Productivity" & Taxonomic_Level == "Phylum")
  
  if(nrow(prod_effects) > 0) {
    edna_prod_plots <- create_edna_env_barplots(
      physeq_obj = physeq_eDNA18S,
      significant_results = prod_effects,
      env_variable = "Productivity",
      model_name = "Full",
      tax_level = "Phylum",
      y_margin = 0.2
    )
    
    if(!is.null(edna_prod_plots)) {
      ggsave(file.path(output_dir, "eDNA_Productivity_Phyla_Effects.pdf"), 
             edna_prod_plots$grid_plot,
             width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 300)
      cat("✓ Saved: eDNA_Productivity_Phyla_Effects.pdf\n")
    }
  }
}

# ============  FINAL SUMMARY =========================

cat("\n", paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("eDNA ANCOMBC2 ANALYSIS COMPLETE\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 70), collapse = ""), "\n")

cat("\nPREVALENCE CUTOFF: 0.2 (taxa present in ≥7 samples = 21%)\n")

cat("\nSITE EFFECTS:\n")
cat("  Significant phyla:", sum(site_phylum_results_edna$diff_abn, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("  Significant families:", sum(site_family_results_edna$diff_abn, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("  Note: Limited power due to small sample size per site (<5)\n")

cat("\nENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS:\n")
cat("  Total significant effects:", nrow(all_env_significant_edna), "\n")
cat("  Depth effects:", sum(all_env_significant_edna$Environmental_Variable == "Depth"), "\n")
cat("  Turbidity effects:", sum(all_env_significant_edna$Environmental_Variable == "Turbidity"), "\n")
cat("  Productivity effects:", sum(all_env_significant_edna$Environmental_Variable == "Productivity"), "\n")
cat("  Robust effects:", sum(all_env_significant_edna$Robust), "\n")

cat("\n✓ All results exported to:", output_dir, "\n")
```
